cards.front: What causes switching delays in a MOSFET inverter?
cards.back: The parasitic capacitance Co at the output node must be charged or discharged through the transistors' ON resistances, creating RC time constants.

cards.front: What is the time constant for the falling edge (output going LOW)?
cards.back: τ₁ = Co × Rsat(T₁), where Rsat(T₁) is the ON resistance of the driver transistor.

cards.front: What is the time constant for the rising edge (output going HIGH)?
cards.back: τ₂ = Co × Rsat(T₂), where Rsat(T₂) is the ON resistance of the load transistor.

cards.front: Why is the rising edge slower than the falling edge in an NMOS inverter?
cards.back: Because Rsat(T₂) > Rsat(T₁). The load transistor (T₂, diode-connected) has a higher ON resistance than the driver (T₁, fully driven), giving a larger time constant.

cards.front: What is the formula for ON resistance (Rsat)?
cards.back: Rsat = VDS(ON) / IDS — the ratio of drain-source voltage when ON to the drain current.

cards.front: What is the steady-state output LOW voltage formula?
cards.back: Vo1 = (Ro / (Ro + Rsat)) × VDD — a voltage divider between the output resistance and the load's ON resistance.
A circuit where the transistor's drain is brought out of the IC without an internal pull-up. An external pull-up resistor must be connected to VDD to define the HIGH state.

cards.front: Why are open-drain outputs used for shared buses?
cards.back: Multiple open-drain outputs can share a bus because they can only pull LOW. There's no push-pull conflict. The bus is HIGH (via pull-up) when all transistors are OFF.

cards.front: What is time division multiplexing on a data bus?
cards.back: Multiple circuits take turns placing their data on a shared bus at different time intervals. Each circuit drives the bus during its allocated time slot while others are OFF.

cards.front: What happens if you omit the pull-up resistor on an open-drain output?
cards.back: The output floats when the transistor is OFF, resulting in an undefined voltage level that can cause unpredictable behaviour in downstream logic.
